## Overview

Explain the Sendbird-style workflow this page represents, then document the OpenIM endpoint or composition that implements it. Mention whether the OpenIM operation is read-only or mutates state.

## Endpoint

`<METHOD> <path>`

Use the exact method and path from the current OpenIM REST API page. If OpenIM has no one-to-one endpoint for this Sendbird-style slot, write `Not one-to-one` and explain the supported OpenIM composition.

| Property       | Value                                                             |
| -------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Product area   | `<application, user, channel, message, moderation, bot, push-notifications, organization, webhook>` |
| Resource       | `<resource name>`                                                 |
| Operation type | `<create, read, update, delete, action, bulk action>`             |
| Auth scope     | `<APP administrator token, admin secret, webhook callback, none>` |
| Idempotent     | `<yes, no, conditional>`                                          |

## Authentication

Describe the required credential and where it is passed. Most OpenIM REST APIs require `operationID` and `token` headers. Token-issuing endpoints such as `get_admin_token` use `operationID` plus request body credentials instead.

```http
operationID: <unique-trace-id>
token: <admin-token>
Content-Type: application/json
```

## Request

### Path parameters

| Parameter | Type     | Required | Description     |
| --------- | -------- | -------- | --------------- |
| `<name>`  | `<type>` | Yes      | `<description>` |

Write `Not applicable.` when the endpoint has no path parameters.

### Query parameters

| Parameter | Type     | Required | Default     | Description     |
| --------- | -------- | -------- | ----------- | --------------- |
| `<name>`  | `<type>` | No       | `<default>` | `<description>` |

Write `Not applicable.` when the endpoint has no query parameters.

### Headers

| Header                           | Required | Description                                |
| -------------------------------- | -------- | ------------------------------------------ |
| `operationID`                    | Yes      | Unique trace ID for this request.          |
| `token`                          | Yes      | APP administrator token when required.     |
| `Content-Type: application/json` | Yes      | Required when the request has a JSON body. |

### Body

| Field     | Type     | Required | Description     |
| --------- | -------- | -------- | --------------- |
| `<field>` | `<type>` | Yes      | `<description>` |

```json
{
  "<field>": "<value>"
}
```

Write `Not applicable.` when the endpoint has no request body.

### Request example

```bash
curl -X <METHOD> 'https://<host><path>' \
  -H 'operationID: <unique-trace-id>' \
  -H 'token: <admin-token>' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"<field>":"<value>"}'
```

## Response

### Success response

Document the exact success envelope and payload for this endpoint.

```json
{
  "errCode": 0,
  "errMsg": "",
  "errDlt": "",
  "data": {}
}
```

### Response fields

| Field     | Type   | Description                   |
| --------- | ------ | ----------------------------- |
| `errCode` | number | OpenIM business status code; `0` means success. |
| `errMsg`  | string | Human-readable error summary.                  |
| `errDlt`  | string | Detailed error information.                     |
| `data`    | object | Endpoint-specific payload.                      |

Write `Not applicable.` when a nested object or list has no additional fields.

## Errors

List HTTP errors and OpenIM business errors separately when both are available. Explain whether each error is retryable.

| HTTP status | `errCode` | Cause     | Retry              |
| ----------- | --------- | --------- | ------------------ |
| `<status>`  | `<code>`  | `<cause>` | `<yes/no and why>` |

## Idempotency and side effects

State whether repeating the same request is safe. Include:

- Which resource state changes.
- Whether repeated calls create duplicates, overwrite fields, or return existing state.
- Whether webhooks, SDK events, counters, unread counts, or audit logs can be emitted.
- Whether the operation is eventually consistent.

## Permissions and limits

Document caller role requirements, target-user restrictions, pagination caps, payload size limits, and validation rules. The current OpenIM REST API docs limit all array request fields to 1000 items.

Write `Not applicable.` for rules that do not apply.

## Verify

- Confirm the endpoint returns the expected `errCode`.
- Confirm the target resource changes or remains unchanged as expected.
- Confirm the corresponding SDK or webhook event when this endpoint emits one.
- Confirm behavior for missing, invalid, and duplicate input.
